stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The traditional Maltese luzzu is a small fishing boat recognizable by its bright paint and protective eye motifs on the bow.
  • Luzzus were used primarily for coastal fishing around the Maltese islands.
  • Contemporary boatbuilders in Malta use both modern and traditional materials when constructing new luzzus.
  • Most components used are traditional, such as locally cut wood and hand-stitched sailcloth.
  • Some components, such as marine-grade epoxies and synthetic ropes, are modern.
stem: The student wants to make a generalization about the materials used in new luzzus. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Hand-stitched sailcloth, a traditional material long used in Maltese fishing boats, is still used by contemporary boatbuilders.
The Maltese luzzu is a small fishing boat used for coastal fishing and recognizable by its bright paint.
Although most materials used in new luzzus are traditional, some modern materials are used.
Contemporary boatbuilders in Malta build new luzzus, traditional small boats with protective eye motifs on the bow.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because it captures the central generalization in the notes: the mix of mostly traditional materials with some modern ones.

Choices A and D give specific facts but not a generalization. Choice B describes the boats themselves rather than the materials.
